key_themes: Record revenue (+25%) and profit growth (+39%) in FY2025
Capital-light, scalable franchise model with resilient recurring lettings revenue
Regulatory tailwinds, M&A opportunities, and new landlord products
Strong cash generation, low debt, and confident outlook
summary: The Property Franchise Group (TPFG.L) released strong full-year 2025 results in mid-March 2026, posting 25% revenue growth to £84.3m and 39% higher adjusted pre-tax profit, alongside a 22% dividend increase and upbeat commentary on organic growth, new compliance products, and Q1 trading.[[1]](https://www.investorschronicle.co.uk/content/7e139d55-c13f-4d30-8bed-5110b1c066f7)[[2]](https://www.insidermedia.com/news/national/strong-organic-growth-and-solid-operational-progress-for-multi-brand-property-franchisor-in-final-results)[[3]](https://thenegotiator.co.uk/news/jump-in-profits-and-revenue-at-the-property-franchise-group/) Investor and analyst reaction has been bullish, praising the capital-light royalty/franchise model, structural rental-market strength, low leverage, and consolidation opportunities in a regulated sector.[[4]](https://x.com/EUnicornHunter/status/2041614758869193182)[[5]](https://x.com/StockwatchUK/status/2033864277543596376) The stock offers a solid dividend yield with long-term outperformance versus the FTSE, though it has pulled back from 2025 highs; overall sentiment is clearly positive with “strong buy” type commentary and noted upside.[[6]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/LON/TPFG/)[[7]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/TPFG.L/)
sources: 10+ web news articles (LSE RNS, Investors Chronicle, Property Wire, Yahoo Finance, MarketBeat, Reuters), 5+ relevant X posts from investors and news accounts (March–April 2026), Company reports, analyst notes (Berenberg Buy), and stock performance data
